response.justification: Bogdan is a highly accomplished software engineer with 19 years of experience and exceptional fundamentals in software craftsmanship, architecture, and testing. However, the role requires extensive C++ expertise, and he has only six months of professional C++ experience (2014-2015 at National Instruments) plus thesis work. While his strong engineering principles and demonstrated ability to learn multiple languages suggest he could eventually excel in the role, the significant gap in primary C++ knowledge and lack of industrial automation domain experience present substantial onboarding challenges for a senior-level position requiring immediate technical productivity.
strengths: 19 years of progressive software engineering experience exceeding the 8-12 year requirement, with proven expertise in designing scalable, high-performance systems and mentoring junior engineers
Exceptional focus on software craftsmanship, TDD, and code quality demonstrated through open-source testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial) and creation of multiple technical workshops
Strong architectural thinking with demonstrated experience in asynchronous I/O, low-latency systems, microservices, event-driven architectures, and DevOps infrastructure (Docker, CI/CD, AWS CDK)
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience—only six months (2014-2015) with most recent decade entirely in JavaScript/TypeScript ecosystem; C++ is the primary technical requirement
No demonstrated background in industrial automation, IoT, manufacturing systems, or IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards that are central to the domain
Missing expertise in multi-threaded systems, message brokers, MQTT, and industrial protocols (Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850) commonly required in automation applications

key_factors.factor: C++ expertise gap (10+ years since last use)
Strong software engineering fundamentals and learning agility
No industrial automation or cybersecurity domain experience
